messages.text: What custom tool would you like to build for this space? (e.g., Kanban board, project dashboard, asset tracker)
create a kanban board to track the project

**Considering Project Ollie Kanban**

I'm now contemplating how to adapt a Kanban board for "Project Ollie". The UX research plan provides initial workflows and the agent interactions to consider. I'm focusing on the baseline workflow aspect and how it can be visualized efficiently within a Kanban system.


**Analyzing Delegation Options**

I'm now delving into delegation strategies, drawing from the "Project Ollie — Overview" slides and the "Research Sprint Readout". The daily sync notes are also providing a good source for recent project context, particularly the June and July entries, for delegation considerations in light of the mandate on coding apps. The June 16th project collaboration session also seems helpful, but I need to focus on delegation implementation.


**Prioritizing Rapid Response**

I'm now zeroing in on the imperative of immediate app delivery. The specifications demand a laser focus on rapid response times. I must skip exploratory steps and tools, and instead immediately output the completed application code. Real-time multiplayer synchronization will make the Kanban board collaborative, fitting the "live meetings" collaborative context. Also, inline scripts and CSS in `index.html` are the default.


**Enforcing Design Compliance**

I'm now fully immersed in enforcing strict adherence to Robert Murdock's Polaris WDS and Material Design 3 specifications. My focus is on eliminating any and all embellishments, sticking to pure minimalism. I'm carefully integrating the WDS structure and spacing variables into the CSS layout. Furthermore, I've specified M3 color tokens for consistency, and embedded the required Google Fonts.


**Defining Typography and Cards**

I'm now nailing down typography and card styles. I've specified Google Sans for headers (16px, weight 500) and Google Sans Text for body (13px-14px, weight 400). Cards will have 16px-18px rounded corners with subtle `#747775` outlines.

\n\n

I'm also identifying tasks to seed the Kanban board. From the Ollie daily sync notes, I'll use "To Do," "In Progress," "In Review," and "Completed" columns. I'm focusing on "Coding Agent (Mike Carcasole)" tasks like bug fixes and walkthroughs, and "Agent Quality & Scheduled Jobs (Cowboy Lynk)" tasks.


**Incorporating Task Integration**

I'm now incorporating tasks from project logs. I'll include "Ollie bugs & improvements" (UX/UI refinements). Next, I'm integrating "Data Privacy & Security Audit" teamfood audits, and, "UXR Track & Co-Design Session" prototypes. Also, I'll include production building block tasks and the engineering acceleration track. The MVP concept, with task inference, team/agent assignment, and draft review, is also integrated.


**Designing Kanban Board**

I'm now detailing the Kanban board's design, focusing on a clean, Workspace-style interface adhering to Material Design 3 and Polaris. I'll use white or light gray backgrounds, Google Sans typography, and eliminate visual clutter for elegant spacing. The system will support real-time multiplayer synchronization via `/api/sync/:envId/:key`, including drag-and-drop for mobile and desktop, and a short-polling sync every 2 seconds. Features will include task creation, editing, deletion, filtering, and cross-device controls.


**Generating Immediate Code**

I'm now focusing on directly outputting completed application code without any unnecessary steps. Following the guidelines, I will immediately output the `index.html` structure in the chat response. I am incorporating the M3 token matrix for theme colors in the CSS style block. I'll adhere to the core principles of immediate code generation and avoiding exploratory tool use.


**Implementing Light-Theme Default**

I'm now implementing the light theme as the default, a neat Workspace-style design that keeps things clean. I'll make the Kanban board self-contained and robust. I am also working on setting the columns as "To Do", "In Progress", "In Review", and "Completed" to get started, and defining the initial tasks to seed the board. For example, "Coding Agent - Build Walkthrough", with assignee Mike Carcasole, is set as an initial task to demonstrate how the board will work.


**Adding Initial Tasks**

I'm now adding specific tasks to the Kanban board. I have added "Fix chat streaming bugs" to the "todo" column under Engineering. Then, I added "Improvements to Scheduled Jobs," with Cowboy Lynk as the assignee, and "UXR In-person Co-design Sessions," with Daniel Giuditta and Mirjam van Esch as assignees. There's also "Data Privacy & Security Audit" and "UX/UI Refinements & Bugs." I need to add internal component building blocks.


**Designing Task Entry**

I am now designing the task entry process, ensuring it's efficient and user-friendly. I've decided on a minimalist approach: clicking an "Add task" card at the bottom of each column will display an inline form, a clear title input, and then an assignee field. I'm focusing on simplicity, ditching any complex modals in favor of seamless flow. I'm supporting a flexible category assignment, with a convenient dropdown.


**Implementing Board Enhancements**

I'm now implementing drag-and-drop functionality for card movement using the HTML5 Drag and Drop API, and adding intuitive buttons on each card to shift statuses. A search/filter bar is going at the top for filtering by assignee, category, or search terms. Real-time syncing with `/api/sync/${envId}/${syncKey}` is in place, short-polling every 2 seconds. I'm adding Google Fonts, Tailwind CSS via CDN, and inline SVG icons for a lean system.


**Confirming Polaris Minimalism**

I'm now confirming my adherence to Robert Murdock's Polaris system guidelines. I'm focusing on "Strict Scope Discipline", "Design Aesthetic", "No Placeholders", "Data Handling & Native Primitives", and the "Zero Embellishment Mandate". I'll eliminate everything unnecessary and deliver code that is clean, simple, and functional, with a focus on minimalistic elegance. I'll also ensure the use of a very light grey style.


**Defining Visual Styling**

I'm now detailing visual styling, specifically the background color and containers. I plan to use `#f8f9fa` or similar for the app container, or use `var(--md-sys-color-surface-container)`, which is `#f0f4f9`. Column containers will have a `var(--md-sys-color-surface-container)` background. White cards will have subtle borders and rounded corners. The top header will be simple with the Workspace app title, search bar, and environment name.


**Refining Initial Tasks**

I'm now refining the initial tasks, adding fallback storage with `localStorage` if the API endpoint is unavailable for a more robust user experience. I'm adding `description` and `category` fields to the task. I will also be creating the initial state structure for the tasks. I've defined a robust initial task state structure. This includes `id`, `title`, and `description`, along with assignee, category, and status.


**Defining Task States**

I'm now defining the structure for the task state. I will use "id," "title," "description," "assignee," "category," and "status" as the core fields. The task state structure also includes "UXR In-person Co-design Sessions," "Data Privacy & Security Audit," and "UX/UI Polish & Improvements," as seed data. I've designed the visual styling for the Kanban board using a Workspace-style approach.


**Implementing Task Interactivity**

I'm now implementing interactive task features. I'm focusing on in-line task creation, drag-and-drop movement, in-place editing (double-click to edit), and task deletion. Tailwind CSS will be included for elegant styling and customized colors aligned with M3 tokens, all via a CDN script. This system must be simple yet fully dynamic.


**Defining Header and Typography**

I'm now focusing on the Kanban board's header and typography, adhering strictly to the Workspace aesthetic and the "Zero Embellishment Mandate." The header will be minimal, displaying "Project Ollie" (Google Sans, weight 500, size 18) and the environment name. I'm also confirming Google Fonts, ensuring their inclusion with the correct weights and styles. I will incorporate color mapping for Tailwind's M3 variables.


**Designing Search Functionality**

I'm now implementing search functionality, ensuring a clean and minimalist design below the header. It will have a subtle outline and light gray background, using Google Sans Text font. I'll include a filter bar with options for assignee and category selection for the Kanban board.


**Styling Task Cards**

I'm now detailing each task card. I'll style with a white background, rounded corners, subtle outlines, and no excessive shadows. The title will use Google Sans, weight 500, size 14px or 15px, with description in Google Sans Text, weight 400, size 13px, using `#474747` text color. I'm adding assignee with size 12px, weight 500 in `#1f1f1f`.


**Implementing Task Forms**

I'm now integrating an in-line form for task creation using input fields for the title, description, and assignee, along with a category selection. Upon saving, the new task updates the board state and triggers `saveSyncedState()`. I am implementing basic HTML5 Drag and Drop for task movement. Also, focus preservation is planned.


**Preventing Unnecessary Re-renders**

I'm now focusing on re-rendering the Kanban board during synchronization, focusing on preventing loss of focus on active inputs. My plan involves comparing the new and existing states, using a simple `JSON.stringify` comparison. I will preserve the user's active input state to ensure a smooth, uninterrupted user experience.


**Preventing Unnecessary Re-renders**

I'm now prioritizing focus management during synchronization to ensure a seamless workflow. I'm focusing on preserving active inputs during board updates. I'll identify and restore the active element using `document.activeElement` or the saved ID, and a simple `JSON.stringify` comparison. I plan to preserve cursor position for any `INPUT` or `TEXTAREA`.


**Implementing Focus Persistence**

I'm now integrating focus persistence, specifically addressing the active element and cursor position. I've designed the code to retrieve and restore focus, including cursor position, ensuring a seamless workflow during synchronizations. I'm focusing on preserving active inputs with new code that compares current and previous states, preserving cursor position. I am incorporating padding and spacing guidelines from Polaris to refine the Kanban layout.


**Refining In-line Task Edit**

I'm now refining the in-line task editing workflow for cards. When a card is in the edit state, inputs appear directly on the card for title, description, assignee, category, and status selection. Save and cancel buttons are provided, keeping the interface clean and interactive. I'm focusing on ensuring this inline experience is both elegant and seamlessly integrated.


**Refining Board Styles**

I'm now refining the board's CSS for a truly Workspace look. The background will be `#f8f9fa` with a clean header using a light border (`#e0e0e0`). I'm adding a pill-shaped search input with subtle gray background and blue border on focus. I'm also adding a minimal workspace indicator and clean filter dropdowns to the top bar. The grid layout will have drag-and-drop capabilities, in-line forms and in-place editing.


**Finalizing Style Refinements**

I'm now finalizing the CSS card and board styling, incorporating the radius of 16px. I'll maintain a clean, Workspace-style aesthetic. The header uses weight 500 for Google Sans, and text is weight 400 for Google Sans Text. The spacing incorporates `--gemini-side-panel-padding-left: 16px;`. Card outlines have `#747775` color with low opacity for subtle borders. The sync code structure is under testing and the localStorage fallback is being tested.


**Refining Sync Logic**

I'm now refining the sync logic. I have implemented focus management to avoid disruption. Comparing state with `JSON.stringify` enables preservation of the active inputs and their cursor positions. I'm focusing on ensuring seamless workflows during synchronizations for uninterrupted user experience.


**Implementing Local State**

I'm now integrating local UI state to enhance the Kanban board's interactivity, specifically managing form interactions during synchronization. The strategy involves `editingTaskId` to track active in-line edits and `addingToColumn` for "Add task" form. A `localEditingState` object that's local to the client ensures uninterrupted user input during syncing. I'm focusing on seamless data integrity by deferring server-state updates when user input is active.


**Implementing Local State Exclusion**

I'm now implementing local state exclusion. To prevent conflicts, I'll bypass remote state updates if either edit or add forms are active, specifically if `localEditingState.editingTaskId !== null` or `localEditingState.addingToColumn !== null`. This method offers seamless user experience with minimal bugs. The system's robust design assures state integrity.


**Adding Task Action Buttons**

I'm now integrating edit and delete buttons into each task card's design. The edit button uses a pencil icon for inline editing with in-place saving and cancellation. The delete button features a trash icon, triggering a simple delete function, after a confirmation prompt if needed. These functionalities improve user interface interaction.


**Refining Card Interactions**

I'm now refining task card interactions. I have added new components. I'm focusing on "Move Task" control, and the logic to adjust the `task.status` by calling `moveTask('${task.id}', 'prev')` and `moveTask('${task.id}', 'next')`. I have included edit and delete buttons. The edit button uses a pencil icon, leading to inline edit. The delete button uses a trash icon, offering a confirmation before removal.


**Integrating Task Interactions**

I'm now integrating the edit and delete task buttons. I've focused on creating the new in-line edit interface, as well as the 'Move Task' controls. This involves the appropriate HTML and logic for an intuitive, streamlined experience.


**Implementing In-line Task Edit**

I'm now integrating in-line task editing and controls within the Kanban board, and adding HTML and logic for a streamlined interface.


**Integrating Task Buttons**

I'm now integrating the provided HTML for task interactions. I'll focus on saving and cancelling in-line edits and building out the "Add Task" form inline, integrating new components.


**Refining Integration**

I'm now integrating the provided HTML for task interactions to create, edit, and delete tasks within the Kanban board, including building the inline "Add Task" form. The focus is on implementing this into the existing layout.


**Refining Move Task Logic**

I'm now refining the logic for the "moveTask" function. I've confirmed `statusOrder` use. Also, the function correctly updates the status for each task with forward and backward direction. I'm focusing on ensuring seamless integration with the existing `saveSyncedState()` and `renderBoard()` functions.


**Implementing Filter Functionality**

I'm now implementing task filtering for enhanced board management. I'll dynamically generate assignee and category lists for filter options. These are now integrated, along with a combined search filter for titles, descriptions, and assignees. The controls are rendered with a clean sub-header with the search.


**Implementing Filter and Sort**

I'm now integrating filtering and sorting into the Kanban board's functionality. I've designed the user interface for filters and the logic required to apply these. I'm focusing on creating clean, intuitive controls for efficient task management.


**Implementing Global "Add Task"**

I'm now integrating a global "Add Task" button, which triggers the task addition form from a new component. This simplifies task creation within the Kanban board, and I am integrating the functionality via a global control for usability.


**Refining Task Entry**

I'm now integrating a global "New Task" button to enhance accessibility. When clicked, it opens the "Add task" card in the "To Do" column and focuses on the title input. I've also written an HTML escape function to prevent XSS. Finally, I'm checking how to render avatars or initials efficiently.


An elegant, real-time collaborative Kanban board has been designed and built specifically for **Project Ollie / Project Friday** tracking. It adheres strictly to Robert Murdock's **Polaris / Workspace Design System (WDS)** and **Material Design 3 (M3)** specifications, utilizing actual project details (like Coding Agents, UXR co-design sessions, and Data Privacy audits) to populate the board with real context.

sandboxFiles.name: [UX Research plan] Project Ollie: Baseline Workflow & Agent Delegation
sandboxFiles.content:
Core Objective
To map the data trails, tool ecosystems, and context-sharing habits of knowledge workers in the real world, as well as their desires and expectations regarding AI delegation.
Participants
* Location & Availability: Must currently live in the U.S. and be able to attend a 90-minute, in-person study session at the NYC Google Office between July 6th and July 24th with their own laptop.1
* Company Profile: Employed at a private educational institute or a commercial business/organization (excluding public education, nonprofits, and government).1
* Software & Tool Usage:
   * Their company must use Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Docs, or Calendar) with official work email addresses.1
   * The "actual work" they do must involve co-editing shared documents and spreadsheets (such as Google Docs or Sheets). Users who primarily manage tasks in dedicated project management tools (like Jira, Asana, or Trello) are disqualified.1
* Role & Authority: Must have a role in their business's decision to purchase digital tools and software (either as a sole decision-maker, part of a collective group, or as an influencer).1
* Collaboration Level: They must spend at least half or more of their work week collaborating with others on projects, tasks, or shared goals.1
Details here: Ollie current needs and painpoints 


Research Questions 
* RQ1 (The Fragmented Truth): What does a knowledge worker's true "to-do list" actually look like across formal tools (Jira, Asana) and informal channels (Slack stars, open browser tabs, mental notes)?
* RQ2 (Context Breadcrumbs): When a user moves a task forward, what specific artifacts (links, documents, specific chat sentences) represent the "proof of work" that Ollie will eventually need to ingest?
* RQ3 (The Translation Gap): How do users naturally summarize a complex day of work into a project update? What details do they filter out, and what do they consider critical?
* RQ4 (Agent Delegation Triggers): What tasks do users actually want to hand off versus what they want to keep control over? What parameters (triggers, boundaries, outputs) define a successful Sojo Agent assignment?
Session script
Artifact & Task Audit (15 mins)
* Goal: Uncover the hidden, informal task ecosystems.
* Activity: Have them open their laptop and show you their current "active stack."
* Prompts to use:
   * "Show me the 3 things you know you have to do today but aren't written down in a formal project board."
   * "Look at your open browser tabs or flagged emails right now—what tasks do those represent?"
   * "Where is the conversation happening about your top priority right now? Can you show me that Slack thread/email chain?"
The "Human Ollie" Walkthrough (25 mins)
* Goal: Treat the user's memory and screen as the database to see what Ollie should have done.
* Activity: Walk backward through their last 24–48 hours of work. Have them literally point at things on their screen.
* Prompts to use:
   * "You opened this Google Doc at 10 AM and left a comment. If Ollie was a smart assistant watching this, what is the 'core project update' it should extract from that action?"
   * "If an AI wrote an update based purely on this Slack thread you just showed me, what would it miss? What's the unwritten context here?"
   * "Look at this completed task. What were all the scattered pieces of data (spreadsheets, links, client feedback) that were required to get it done?"
AI Agents tasks (15 mins)
* Goal: Use their real data to define the scope and boundaries of the agents.
* Activity: Review the real tasks identified in Phase 1 and 2. Print out blank "Sojo Agent Job Description" cards (or use a digital whiteboard) and fill them out together.
* Prompts to use:
   * "Look at this specific task you spent an hour on yesterday. If an AI Agent could have done it, what is the exact trigger to start it? (e.g., 'When a new CSV lands in this folder' or 'When a client pings me via email')."
   * "What does the agent need access to on your laptop to successfully do this?"
   * "What is the exact output you expect? A draft email? A spreadsheet update? And where should it put it?"

      * Projects frequently originate in live meetings or email/chat communications.
      * Workers prefer asynchronous methods like chat or email to clarify vague tasks.
      * Shared documents are the primary environment for actual collaborative work.
      * Task tracking is often manual, fragmented across tools, or reliant on memory
      * Progress updates are typically manual and ad-hoc, often depending on informal syncs
      * Teams predominantly perform manual verification of AI-generated content instead of using automation.
